finding.summary: Track is well-recorded with no FX chain; tonal balance appears natural but spectrum data is sparse, limiting diagnostic confidence.
finding.probable_cause: No processing applied; natural tonal balance of a clean guitar recording.
finding.confidence_reason: Isolation is verified, silence fraction negligible, and all measured levels are within typical ranges for a clean guitar. However, only three 1/3-octave bands are available, restricting spectral analysis.

evidence_refs.description: Peak headroom of 6 dB below full scale, safe for mixing.
True peak at -5.5 dB, no intersample peaks.
Crest factor 12 dB, moderate for a clean guitar.
Integrated LUFS -20, suitable for raw tracks.
Mid RMS -18 dB, strong center image.
Side RMS -30 dB, 12 dB below mid, typical for a centered instrument.
100 Hz at -18 dB, slight low-end emphasis.

proposal.reason: No actionable issue is supported by the available data; track is clean and requires no immediate adjustment. Further spectral detail would be needed to justify EQ or other moves.
